evals.prompt: I need to add GDPR cookie consent to my Django project. The project is in evals/files/sample-django-project (relative to the skill directory). Set it up with Essential and Performance/Analytics sections — I use Google Analytics 4. My domain is .mysite.com. The wheel file doesn't exist yet so skip the installation step and just configure everything. Use 'center' for the dialog position.
evals.expected_output: The settings.py has gdpr_cookie_consent in INSTALLED_APPS and a complete COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS with essential and performance sections. The urls.py includes the cookie_consent URLs. The base.html has the CSS link and widget include. A conditional template for performance cookies exists with a GA4 script placeholder.
expectations: gdpr_cookie_consent is added to INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py
COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS is defined in settings.py with at least two sections: essential and performance
The essential section has required=True
The performance section has a conditional_html_template_name pointing to a template
The cookie consent URL pattern is added to urls.py with namespace='cookie_consent'
The base.html includes the gdpr-cookie-consent CSS link
The base.html includes the cookie_consent.html widget template
A conditional HTML template file is created for the performance section

evals.prompt: Help me set up django-gdpr-cookie-consent in my Django project at evals/files/sample-django-project-with-ga. I need Essential + Performance cookie sections. My site domain is .example.com. Skip the wheel installation step. The project already has Google Analytics in the base template — please handle that properly.
evals.expected_output: The settings.py is updated with INSTALLED_APPS and COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS. The urls.py gets the cookie consent routes. The base.html gets the CSS/widget added. The existing GA script in base.html is moved to the conditional performance template. The user is informed about what was moved.
expectations: gdpr_cookie_consent is added to INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py
COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS is defined with essential and performance sections
The cookie consent URL pattern is added to urls.py
The base.html includes the gdpr-cookie-consent CSS link
The base.html includes the cookie_consent.html widget include
The existing Google Analytics script (G-ABC123XYZ9) is detected and flagged or moved to the conditional performance template
A conditional HTML template for performance section is created

evals.prompt: Set up django-gdpr-cookie-consent for the Django project at evals/files/sample-django-project. I need all four sections: Essential, Functionality, Performance, and Marketing. Domain is .shop.example.com. Skip wheel installation. Put the dialog at the bottom of the screen.
evals.expected_output: Settings.py gets all four cookie sections in COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS. The dialog_position is set to 'bottom'. All three non-essential sections have conditional_html_template_name. Three conditional template files are created. URLs and base template are updated.
expectations: gdpr_cookie_consent is added to INSTALLED_APPS
COOKIE_CONSENT_SETTINGS contains four sections: essential, functionality, performance, marketing
dialog_position is set to 'bottom'
All three non-essential sections have conditional_html_template_name defined
Cookie consent URLs are added to urls.py
base.html has the CSS link and widget include
Three conditional template files are created (functionality, performance, marketing)
